name: MAS Boston Society
mission: Tarbiya Department Tarbiya aims at rooting the faith, purifying the soul, expanding and refining knowledge/understanding, developing and refining all aspects of character, boosting commitment, building skills, and instilling the discipline necessary to conduct a successful, fulfilling, meaningful, and balanced life of learning, virtue, commitment, and service of Islam and humanity.
theoryOfChange: The 'TARBIYA' page on the MAS Boston Society website outlines the organization's core competency and approach to fostering change. It describes the MAS Tarbiya Program as aiming to root faith, purify the soul, expand knowledge, develop character, boost commitment, build skills, and instill discipline for a meaningful life of learning, virtue, commitment, and service of Islam and humanity. The document explicitly states, "MAS's vision is that individual bring about real change in a society and therefore moving them towards the goal of constant self-development and the development of those around them is absolutely essential. Tarbiya is a life-long process for change; this change starts with every individual and extends to the whole society." This serves as a strategic framework detailing how their programs are intended to create individual and societal impact.

grantsData.purpose: MONEY COLLECTED AS PART OF ZAKAT (AN EXPECTATION IN THE MUSLIM FAITH THAT ALL FINANCIALLY STABLE MUSLIMS PAY MONEY TOWARDS HELPING NEEDY INDIVIDUALS IN THE COMMUNITY) IS GIVEN TO THE ISLAMIC CIRCLE OF NORTH AMERICA (ICNA) RELIEF, WHO THEN DISTRIBUTES THIS MONEY BASED ON AN APPLICATION AND REVIEW PROCESS TO NEEDY INDIVIDUALS.

PAYMENT TO ISB (A RELIGOUS ORGANIZATION) FOR ISB TO PAYDOWN LOAN WHICH WAS USED TO FINANCE THE CONTRUCTION OF THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF BOSTON CULTURAL CENTER (THE CENTER). THIS IS CONSISTENT WITH THE MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT BETWEEN MAS BOSTON AND ISB FOR THE CENTER.

MONEY COLLECTED AS PART OF ZAKAT (AN EXPECTATION IN THE MUSLIM FAITH THAT ALL FINANCIALLY STABLE MUSLIMS PAY MONEY TOWARDS HELPING NEEDY INDIVIDUALS IN THE COMMUNITY) IS GIVEN TO THE ISLAMIC CIRCLE OF NORTH AMERICA (ICNA) RELIEF, WHO THEN DISTRIBUTES THIS MONEY BASED ON AN APPLICATION AND REVIEW PROCESS TO NEEDY INDIVIDUALS.
PAYMENT TO ISB FOR ISB TO PAY DOWN THE CULTURAL CENTER (MOSQUE) LOAN.

dimension_explanations.alignment: MAS Boston offers high donor fit for the Muslim community by maintaining zakat eligibility and providing specific giving channels for religious obligations [1][3]. The mission addresses urgent religious and cultural needs through civic engagement and youth development [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization shows high directness in capacity building through its Tarbiya department, which focuses on character development and service [4]. Financially, it operates efficiently with a fundraising cost of $0.05 per $1 raised and allocates 85.3% of its $3,286,832 revenue to programs [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Muslim American Society - MAS Boston empowers the Muslim community through comprehensive spiritual development, youth leadership, and civic engagement.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 85.3%, indicating most funds go directly to services
Strong fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.05 per $1 raised
Zakat-eligible status with clear donation pathways for religious giving
baseline_narrative.summary: MAS Boston is a non-profit organization dedicated to personal and community development through its Tarbiya, Youth, and Public Affairs departments [4]. The organization reported a total revenue of $3,286,832 and maintains a high program expense ratio of 85.3% [2][4].

alignment.explanation: MAS Boston offers <cite id="1">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serves the <cite id="1">masakin (needy) asnaf category</cite>, making it a high fit for Muslim donors.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust the organization due to its <cite id="4">independent board governance</cite> and <cite id="2">verified financial reporting</cite> through official tax filings.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be enhanced by publishing annual impact reports that include specific beneficiary data and audited financial statements.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value local community institution-building and want to support spiritual and leadership development through a zakat-eligible framework.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations through a trusted local institution
Believe in the importance of youth leadership and spiritual 'Tarbiya'
Value high financial efficiency and low fundraising overhead
Support the development of American Muslim civic engagement
giving_considerations: Donations support a mix of direct programming and local grantmaking
The organization focuses on long-term character building rather than just emergency relief
Financial data is stable but reserves are leaner than some national charities
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international emergency relief or those who require granular cost-per-beneficiary metrics for every dollar spent.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has a verified theory of change and strong program ratios, though it lacks external RCT-level evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization utilizes a 'Tarbiya' model to root faith and develop character, which then translates into youth leadership and civic engagement.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that spiritual and leadership programs are effectively translating into community benefit.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 13.4% CAGR and expanding programs in Boston, the organization has significant capacity to absorb new funding for its PACE and Youth initiatives.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="2">program expense ratio of 85.3%</cite>, which exceeds the industry standard of 75% for religious and cultural non-profits.
MAS Boston demonstrates fiscal discipline by spending only <cite id="2">$0.05 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
The charity provides clear pathways for <cite id="1">zakat-eligible giving</cite>, supporting categories such as the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin).

strengths_deep_dive: MAS Boston's financial health is underscored by its ability to maintain <cite id="2">3.2 months of working capital</cite>, providing a buffer for its diverse programming. The organization's revenue model is well-diversified, with <cite id="4">$796,962 generated from program service revenue</cite> alongside its $2.4 million in contributions. This financial stability supports a robust leadership structure where <cite id="4">officer compensation is kept at a modest $91,461</cite>, representing a small fraction of total organizational expenditure.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Society - MAS Boston serves as a cornerstone for community development in the Greater Boston area, focusing on spiritual growth, youth leadership, and civic engagement. The organization operates with a significant financial footprint, reporting <cite id="4">total revenue of $3,286,832</cite> in its most recent comprehensive filing. MAS Boston distinguishes itself through a high level of financial efficiency, directing an <cite id="2">85.3% program expense ratio</cite> toward its core initiatives, which include the Tarbiya Department, Youth Development, and Public Affairs & Civic Engagement (PACE). This efficiency is further highlighted by its <cite id="2">fundraising efficiency of $0.05 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions are utilized for direct service delivery rather than administrative overhead. Beyond its own internal programming, the organization acts as a community anchor through strategic grantmaking, providing <cite id="4">$332,783 in domestic grants</cite> to key local institutions such as the Islamic Society of Boston and the Islamic Circle of North America. By integrating spiritual education with practical community service, MAS Boston seeks to develop American Muslim leaders who are both grounded in their faith and actively engaged in societal improvement.
